target: WIN-038 defense neutralization — preempt the 'quarter-wave doesn't apply at planetary scale / Schumann is transverse mode' defense rated defense_survives=3 by curmudgeon c3 advocate. Inserts ONE new paragraph into the existing detail_evidence; all four existing paragraphs remain unchanged.
insertion_point: In data/wins.json WIN-038.detail_evidence, insert the new paragraph immediately AFTER the third paragraph (the 'Dome's own triple-count' paragraph ending with the cross-reference sentence shown in `insertion_anchor_tail`) and BEFORE the fourth paragraph ('Stability is ionospheric physics, not dome evidence.'). This positions the preemptive rebuttal after the structural self-contradiction argument and before the standalone stability-is-textbook argument, so the reader sees the escape hatch closed in sequence with the rest of the impossibility case.
insertion_anchor_tail: See Section 2.1 for the full pole-floor and supremum-impossibility treatment, and WIN-029 / WIN-002 for the duplicate-counting cross-reference.
insertion_html: The "quarter-wave doesn't apply at planetary scale" escape is closed in advance (preemptive). The strongest available defense against the pole-floor argument is to say f = c/(4H) is a parallel-plate simplification that breaks down at planetary scale, and the real Schumann mechanism is a transverse (TM) mode running around the cavity's horizontal dimension: fn = (c / 2πR) × √(n(n+1)) ≈ 10.59 Hz for n = 1 on a 6,371 km sphere, with the observed 7.83 Hz produced by ionospheric damping. This is correct globe physics — and it cannot rescue the dome for three compounding reasons. (i) The dome is already on record using quarter-wave. The model page (V50.6, “Schumann Cavity Height”) writes H = c / (4 × f_Schumann) = 299,792 / (4 × 7.83) = 9,572 km, and WIN-029 is the credit claim for that exact inversion. A retreat to transverse modes does not save WIN-038 in isolation; it requires the author to simultaneously withdraw WIN-029 and delete the 9,572 km firmament-height figure propagated throughout the model. You cannot have quarter-wave for the back-derivation you want and transverse-mode for the forward-derivation you need. (ii) Transverse mode imports a globe fingerprint. The spherical transverse formula reproduces 7.83 Hz only when the wavelength-setting length equals Earth's circumference, 2πREarth = 40,030 km. The dome's published disc diameter is 2 × 20,015 = 40,030 km — identical to four significant figures, because disc_radius = π × REarth is exactly the azimuthal-equidistant projection identity. Plugging dome disc diameter into the globe's transverse formula is the classic tautology flagged in Part 6 (STEELMAN-03: Tesla Disc Diameter Lock): a number built from globe geometry by construction cannot independently confirm dome geometry. (iii) The dome's non-uniform H(r) violates the transverse-mode boundary conditions. The fn ≈ 10.59 Hz formula assumes a closed, approximately uniform spherical shell; the dome's H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r / 8,619) km varies by a factor of four across the disc and terminates at an open rim. Borrowing the sphere's mode formula without the sphere's geometry is precisely the silent formula-switch already documented in Section 2.1. There is no configuration in which the dome keeps its own geometry, uses a mode formula it has not committed to elsewhere, and arrives at 7.83 Hz — either the pole-floor holds (quarter-wave) or the globe is assumed (transverse).
holes_addressed: advocate_mode.best_defense rated defense_survives=3 — transverse-mode retreat from quarter-wave
Surviving defense summarized in expansion-tracker EXP-198 notes ('quarter-wave formula f=c/(4H) applies only to small resonators, not planetary-scale cavities')

Curmudgeon c3 preemptive_recommendation points (1)-(3): dome commits to quarter-wave via model-page line 64; disc diameter = Earth circumference fingerprint; non-uniform H(r) breaks transverse-mode boundary conditions
new_evidence_added: raw-text/05-model.txt line 64: explicit dome commitment to H = c/(4 × f_Schumann) = 9,572 km — pinned to WIN-029
Azimuthal-equidistant projection identity: disc_radius = π × R_Earth ⇒ disc_diameter = 2πR = globe circumference (40,030 km match to 4 sig figs)
Cross-reference to Part 6 STEELMAN-03 (Tesla Disc Diameter Lock, <details id='p6-610-9'>) where the disc-diameter-by-construction argument is already developed

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'f = c/(4H) in the model page is a shorthand height tag, not a mode formula. WIN-029 is just restating the observation as an equivalent height — we never claimed quarter-wave was the resonance mechanism.'
anticipated_objections.response: Then WIN-029's claim of 'cavity-height confirmation' collapses to tautology — it is quoting the observation back in different units. The score on WIN-029 would drop from 'confirmed prediction' to 'unit conversion,' and the dome must publicly withdraw it. Either commit to the formula and accept the pole-floor, or retract the claim.

anticipated_objections.objection: '10.59 Hz is the undamped globe value; maybe the dome's different geometry gives a different undamped value that happens to damp to 7.83 Hz too.'
anticipated_objections.response: Possible, but the dome has not produced any such derivation. The only Schumann-to-geometry mapping in the dome's source material is the quarter-wave one. A hypothetical future dome transverse-mode derivation is not the claim on the scoreboard today. Per the Part 3.1b post-hoc standard, unspecified future derivations do not retroactively convert a 74-year-old measurement into a dome prediction.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'The 40,030 km disc-diameter match to 40,075 km globe circumference is coincidental — the dome's geometry was not fitted to this number.'
anticipated_objections.response: disc_radius was tuned via test_curve_stretching.py and find_curve.py (see Part 6 / Section 2.9) against globe-geodesic inputs; an AE projection is fitted by construction. The ~0.01% match is not independent of the globe; it IS the globe, projected.

kernel_of_truth: The advocate is right that Schumann resonance is physically a transverse TM mode around Earth's circumference, not a vertical quarter-wave standing wave. The quarter-wave formula is indeed a parallel-plate simplification — globe-physics textbooks use the spherical-harmonic transverse formula. The dome defender's physics intuition about which mode actually produces 7.83 Hz is correct. What the defense misses is that this observation is fatal rather than helpful: the dome cannot use the transverse-mode formula without adopting the globe's geometry (spherical, closed, uniform shell of radius R = circumference / 2π = 6,371 km), and it cannot use the quarter-wave formula without falling below its own pole-floor. The correct Schumann physics and the dome's stated geometry are mutually exclusive. Acknowledging the transverse-mode objection and showing it fails is stronger than denying the objection.

defense_neutralization.notes: Agree with curmudgeon rating. Defense is real physics and would be rhetorically effective if left unaddressed. Preemptive paragraph closes all three escape routes: (a) dome's own WIN-029 commits it to quarter-wave; (b) transverse formula requires globe circumference, which disc diameter replicates by AE-projection construction; (c) dome's varying H(r) violates the transverse mode's uniform-shell boundary condition. Paragraph is an insertion, not a full rewrite — the three existing substantive paragraphs and the stability paragraph survive unchanged.
